headline: Ministerial asset declarations still voluntary after 25 years of promises
context: Despite pledges from every government since 1999, ministerial asset declarations remain voluntary with no verification mechanism or public disclosure requirement.

cards.big: 25 years of promising mandatory asset declarations — still voluntary for ministers
cards.sub: Every PM since Mahathir's first tenure has pledged this reform without delivering

cards.big: MACC Act Section 36 requires only 'prescribed officers' to declare assets
cards.sub: Cabinet ministers are not classified as prescribed officers under current regulations

cards.big: Indonesia's KPK publishes asset declarations for all 575 DPR members online
cards.sub: Regional neighbours demonstrate political asset transparency is operationally feasible

cards.big: Only 7 of 31 current cabinet members voluntarily disclosed assets in 2025
cards.sub: 77% of ministers chose not to disclose despite PM Anwar's transparency pledge

cards.big: Voluntary asset declaration is an honesty test — and 77% of ministers chose not to take it
Should serving ministers be legally required to publish verified asset declarations annually?
